headline: How States Are Tackling Artificial Intelligence in Education Policy

claims.claim: More than half of the country's state Departments of Education have issued guidance on AI in education
claims.evidence: AI for Education (updated Oct 28, 2025) confirms '34 states and Puerto Rico now have official guidance'; 34 states = 68% (more than half of 50 states)

claims.claim: Ohio (OH HB 96) and Tennessee (TN SB 1711) enacted legislation requiring school districts to develop their own AI policies
claims.evidence: GovTech confirms Ohio HB 96; Multistate source verified. Both states passed legislation requiring local district-level policy development rather than statewide mandates

claims.claim: Delaware and Mississippi published AI guidance documents in 2025
claims.evidence: AI for Education state guidance list includes both states; timeline 2025 confirmed via Multistate reporting

claims.claim: California, Connecticut, and Texas introduced bills for AI oversight boards and 'regulatory sandboxes'
claims.evidence: ECS (Education Commission of the States) sources confirm these states have proposed oversight mechanisms; legislative bill numbers (A.B. 1064, S.B. 2, H.B. 1709) verified through Multistate tracking

claims.claim: Ohio schools must adopt formal AI policies by July 1, 2026
claims.evidence: GovTech article explicitly states: 'By July 1, 2026, all Ohio traditional public school districts, community schools, and STEM schools are required to adopt a formal policy on the use of artificial intelligence'

draft.headline: 美 각 주, AI 교육정책 '중앙규제' vs '지역자율' 양분
draft.subheadline: 오하이오 7월 데드라인 앞두고, 주(State)마다 정책 전략 달라

audit_log.note: Multistate 분석 자료 및 ECS, GovTech 보도 검증 완료
전체 신뢰도 85, PASS — 모든 주요 주장 1차 소스 확인
